CheckTitle: Security group restricts SQL Server TCP access from the internet
Description: Checks that SQL Server TCP ports 1433 and 1434 are not exposed to unrestricted IPv4 (`0.0.0.0/0`) or IPv6 (`::/0`) sources.
Risk: Public SQL Server access enables credential attacks, service discovery, and database exploitation, risking data and service compromise.

Code.CLI:
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 1433/1433 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 1433/1433 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 1434/1434 --SourceCidrIp 0.0.0.0/0
aliyun ecs RevokeSecurityGroup --RegionId <region_id> --SecurityGroupId <security_group_id> --IpProtocol tcp --PortRange 1434/1434 --Ipv6SourceCidrIp ::/0
Code.Other: Restrict SQL Server TCP ingress for both `SourceCidrIp` and `Ipv6SourceCidrIp` to application hosts or trusted CIDRs.

Recommendation.Text: Do not expose TCP ports 1433 or 1434 to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
